name: planning-framework
description: Apply structured thinking before coding. Use when: starting new features, making architectural decisions, refactoring large components, or evaluating implementation approaches. Includes Musk's 5-step algorithm and ICE scoring framework.
capabilities: Apply Musk's 5-step algorithm (question requirements, delete, simplify, accelerate, automate)
Calculate ICE scores to prioritize features by Impact, Confidence, and Ease
Evaluate feature requests to identify simpler solutions before coding
Identify unnecessary code and dependencies using systematic deletion review
Create planning checklists before starting development tasks
Assess technical debt and refactoring needs using structured frameworks
author: 5MinFutures
securitySummary: This skill is a pure Markdown documentation file containing planning frameworks. No executable code exists in either file. All static findings are false positives: command strings like 'npm uninstall' and 'tsc --noEmit' are documentation examples, not shell execution. No file system access, network calls, environment variables, or external commands are present.
valueStatement: Developers often start coding without proper planning, leading to scope creep and technical debt. This framework provides Musk's 5-step algorithm and ICE scoring to systematically evaluate features before writing any code.
limitations: Does not execute code or modify project files automatically
Does not integrate with project management tools or issue trackers
Does not provide time estimates for complex features
Does not access codebase to analyze existing architecture

useCases.title: Plan Features Systematically
useCases.description: Use ICE scoring and Musk's framework before starting any feature over 50 lines of code.

useCases.title: Prioritize Backlog Items
useCases.description: Evaluate feature requests using Impact, Confidence, and Ease to make data-driven prioritization decisions.

useCases.title: Review Technical Debt
useCases.description: Identify refactoring candidates and cleanup opportunities using structured deletion and simplification criteria.

promptTemplates.title: Quick Feature Eval
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the ICE scoring framework to evaluate this feature: [describe feature]. Calculate Impact, Confidence, and Ease scores (1-10 each), then compute the ICE score and recommend priority.

promptTemplates.title: Delete Review
promptTemplates.prompt: Apply Musk's Step 2 (Delete) to this codebase section: [paste code]. What can be eliminated? What dependencies are unused? What logic serves no clear purpose?

promptTemplates.title: Architecture Decision
promptTemplates.prompt: Use Musk's 5-step algorithm to evaluate this architectural decision: [describe decision]. Question the requirements, identify what can be deleted, and determine if automation is appropriate.

promptTemplates.title: Refactoring Plan
promptTemplates.prompt: Apply the planning checklist to refactor this component: [paste code >200 lines]. Identify extraction points, estimate effort, and create a step-by-step refactoring plan using the ICE framework.

bestPractices: Always complete Steps 1-3 (Question, Delete, Simplify) before starting any feature implementation
Calculate ICE scores for all features over 100 lines of code before prioritizing
Multiply initial time estimates by 2 to account for edge cases and testing
antiPatterns: Starting to code before questioning requirements and checking for simpler solutions
Using 'any' types for quick implementation (technical debt accumulates)
Adding code 'for later' that never gets used (YAGNI - You Aren't Gonna Need It)

faq.question: What AI tools support this skill?
faq.answer: Works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. Provides structured prompts for planning conversations.

faq.question: How accurate are ICE scores?
faq.answer: ICE scoring is subjective. Use it to compare relative priorities within your team, not as absolute metrics.

faq.question: Can this integrate with Jira or Linear?
faq.answer: No. This is a prompt framework. Copy ICE scores and recommendations to your project management tool manually.

faq.question: Is my data safe?
faq.answer: Yes. This skill contains only prompts and documentation. No code execution, file access, or data collection occurs.

faq.question: Why is Step 2 'Delete' before Step 3 'Simplify'?
faq.answer: Deleting unnecessary parts first makes simplification easier. Remove what does not belong before improving what remains.

faq.question: How does this compare to other planning methods?
faq.answer: Combines Musk's engineering philosophy with ICE prioritization. More actionable than user stories, more structured than ad-hoc planning.
